mnemonics: M.U.S.T. Design: Match the User's System of Thought. Mental models are the user's internal system for understanding how things work. Your design MUST match it.
keyPoints: A mental model is the user's internal representation of how a system works.
Conceptual design involves creating a high-level, user-centered structure for a product.
The goal is to align the system's design (the conceptual model) with the user's mental model to create an intuitive experience.
commonMistakes: Designing based on the developer's mental model instead of the user's.
Overcomplicating the conceptual design with technical jargon and structures unfamiliar to the user.
Failing to test and refine the conceptual design with real users to check for alignment.
